prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of phenol (13 atoms, neutral singlet) completed via DFT single-point calculation at B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard'), gas phase, in PySCF. The parent neutral, cation (+1 doublet), and anion (−1 doublet) states all converged cleanly. Fukui descriptors (nucleophilicity f⁻, electrophilicity f⁺, dual descriptor ΔF) were computed from Mulliken partial charges. Key results: ortho carbons (atoms 3 and 4) are the most electrophilic sites (f⁺ = 2.10, 1.62) for nucleophilic attack; the hydroxyl oxygen (atom 1) is the most nucleophilic (f⁻ = 0.162) for electrophilic attack. Charge-conservation integrity checks passed (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000). All cation and anion SCF converged. Input geometry: user-supplied .xyz. Calculation command: chemkit fukui --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 /phenol.xyz.
